title: Retire cluster membership polling with ordered events
blocked_by: PARTICIPANT-CONTRACT-R15-GATE — final participant-contract content hash and both keys are required before dispatch; the stable law/socket core was read only from f9f9bc2:docs/design/PARTICIPANT-CONTRACT.md:135-153,2776-2828, and every later contract-fixture number, equality, or budget is PENDING-CONTRACT-HASH until this gate passes.

BEAMR-ASK-ATOMIC-INITIAL-VIEW-ORDERED-DELTAS — ConnectionManager must expose an atomic initial membership view plus ordered join/leave continuation with defined duplicate, coalescing, overflow, close, and shutdown semantics; current liminal only snapshots `connected_nodes()` at `crates/liminal-server/src/cluster/membership.rs:112-128` (banked scout pack, reverified at fe3f7ec).
design_anchor: B. Brief grouping proposal for post-key-turn server briefs
purpose: SKELETON — NOT DISPATCH-READY. Define the standalone membership-event-source retirement selected by `LAW1-POLLING-RETIREMENT.md` section `B. Brief grouping proposal for post-key-turn server briefs`: replace beamr connected-node snapshots, set diffs, stop-flag sampling, and sleep with an atomic initial view and ordered pushed deltas. The banked scout pack reverified every cited membership range at fe3f7ec; contract-fixture values are intentionally absent and remain PENDING-CONTRACT-HASH.
task: Draft structure and acceptance intent only. Consume a future beamr `ConnectionManager` event API, preserve the startup view, ordered join/leave effects, R5 peer-join backfill, beamr's already-occupied synchronous node-down purge hook, and explicit shutdown. Scope is `crates/liminal-server/src/cluster/membership.rs`, `crates/liminal-server/src/cluster/sync.rs`, runtime wiring, and focused unit/real-cluster tests. Do not choose an upstream API, backend, buffering policy, or contract-fixture budget before the named gates close. All tree claims are attributed to the LAW-1 paper and banked scout pack's fe3f7ec citation-drift pass.

requirements.title: Hold the contract gate and keyed socket register
requirements.spec:
Keep socket references in this additive keyed register; r15 additions SHALL append rows without restructuring R2-R6.

| key | status at skeleton pin | required use |
|---|---|---|
| `«MEMBERSHIP-EVENT-SOURCE»` | genuinely open at f9f9bc2 contract line 2802 | Require atomic initial membership plus ordered deltas and explicit source shutdown; no snapshot cadence qualifies. |
| `FENCED: DCR-RECOVERY-ARM` | open r15 mandate — do not draft against | No requirement depends on `DetachedCredentialRecovery` or `«RETENTION-UNITS»` at f9f9bc2 lines 1738-1740 and 2795; when r15 resolves the non-fireable recovery continuation, any membership/fate interaction slots here as an added row. |
| `<future-r15-socket-key>` | additive slot | Append any new keyed socket, including a TS-pressure Defer-retirement socket, without renumbering or reshaping this brief. |

Every later fixture-derived number, equality, or budget SHALL be labelled `PENDING-CONTRACT-HASH`, cite f9f9bc2 as provisional provenance, and remain explicitly unstable until the participant-contract gate passes.
acceptance: The socket register remains keyed and a new r15 socket can be appended without changing R2-R6.
No dependency, requirement, or verification criterion relies on the DCR recovery arm.
A source review finds no unmarked participant-contract fixture-derived number, equality, or budget; every such later insertion is labelled PENDING-CONTRACT-HASH and provisional to f9f9bc2.

requirements.title: Specify the atomic initial-view and ordered-delta handoff
requirements.spec: The upstream event-source contract SHALL atomically return an initial membership view and a continuation that cannot lose a join or leave across handoff. It SHALL define order, duplication, coalescing, overflow, backend close/error, resubscription, and explicit shutdown. It SHALL NOT expose a timer-driven snapshot/diff API as event delivery. The current limitation is pinned at `crates/liminal-server/src/cluster/membership.rs:32,112-128`, and the exact ask is in `LAW1-POLLING-RETIREMENT.md:153-183,565,569-573` (banked scout pack reverified at fe3f7ec).
acceptance: A join or leave concurrent with initial-view acquisition appears exactly in the defined initial-or-delta side of the handoff and is not lost.
Rapid join, leave, and rejoin are observed in the selected ordering with explicitly tested duplicate/coalescing behavior.
Backend close, overflow, error, and local shutdown each produce a defined terminal or recovery outcome without a sampling fallback.

requirements.title: Consume membership deltas and explicit shutdown
requirements.spec: WHEN the selected source yields its initial view or an ordered membership delta, THE SYSTEM SHALL apply that fact through the existing membership consumer. WHILE the backend and local owner are silent, THE SYSTEM SHALL remain blocked until a delta, source fate, or explicit shutdown. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT invoke `poll_once`, diff `connected_nodes()` snapshots, sample a stop flag, or sleep. The retirement family is `crates/liminal-server/src/cluster/membership.rs:40-44,112-128,198-230` (LAW-1 paper section `2. Cluster membership PollLoop`; banked scout pack reverified at fe3f7ec).
acceptance: Startup establishes membership from the atomic initial view before consuming the continuation.
Explicit shutdown wakes and joins the consumer even when no membership event is pending.
A stable backend performs no repeated table snapshot, tracked-set diff, consumer wake, or stop-flag sample after subscription.

requirements.title: Preserve join backfill and the existing leave purge hook
requirements.spec: WHEN an ordered join delta is applied, THE SYSTEM SHALL retain `ClusterSync::on_peer_join` subscription re-advertisement at `crates/liminal-server/src/cluster/sync.rs:241-247`. WHEN a leave delta is observed, THE SYSTEM SHALL preserve logging/consumer semantics without replacing or competing with beamr's scheduler-owned synchronous pg purge hook at `crates/liminal-server/src/cluster/membership.rs:5-22`; `ClusterSync::on_peer_leave` remains downstream at `crates/liminal-server/src/cluster/sync.rs:249-260`. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT infer that the existing leave hook supplies join ordering or the atomic initial handoff (banked scout pack, reverified at fe3f7ec).
acceptance: A late join receives existing local subscription advertisements through the pushed join event path.
Node-down still purges remote members synchronously through beamr's occupied hook, independent of consumer scheduling.
Join/leave logging and peer inspection remain correct without a periodic tracked-set diff.

requirements.title: Replace snapshot-cadence tests with event barriers
requirements.spec: Rewrite current tests so membership transitions are released and observed through deterministic source barriers. Tombstone `first_poll_of_empty_table_yields_no_peers` and its direct `poll_once` contract at `crates/liminal-server/src/cluster/membership.rs:391-403`. Rewrite the real-cluster eventual helper at `crates/liminal-server/tests/cluster_two_node.rs:26-39` and the late-join cadence wording at `:228-247` while retaining backfill and node-drop outcomes (banked scout pack, reverified at fe3f7ec).
acceptance: The initial empty view is covered through the new source contract rather than a direct `poll_once` unit test.
Late join backfill starts from an injected/delivered join barrier and retains its cross-node subscription assertion.
Node-drop cleanup remains independently covered as the synchronous beamr purge behavior, not as evidence for a membership poll.

requirements.title: Prove membership quiescence and remove the poll family
requirements.spec: Add counter-backed quiescence coverage following the park-flip inversion precedent at `crates/liminal-server/src/server/connection/process_wake_tests.rs:43-65`. Delete the poll constant, snapshot change-detection role, `PollLoop`, and stop/load/sleep implementation at `crates/liminal-server/src/cluster/membership.rs:40-44,112-128,198-230`. Any by-design backend watcher or helper cost SHALL receive an evidence-derived bound after the contract gate and SHALL NOT be filled with a skeleton default.
acceptance: `stable_membership_source_has_zero_consumer_wakes` arms the source, holds the backend stable, and asserts source-snapshot and consumer-wake counters remain flat until shutdown.
A source search finds no production membership-family `PollLoop`, `POLL_INTERVAL`, `poll_once`, or `run_poll_loop` change-detection path.
No hidden heartbeat, scan, or timer is accepted as the source of membership change.

boundaries: SHALL NOT dispatch before the participant-contract r15 content hash and two-key gate close; f9f9bc2 is provisional normative-core evidence, not implementation authority.

SHALL NOT introduce an unmarked participant-contract fixture-derived number, equality, or budget; label each such later value PENDING-CONTRACT-HASH, cite f9f9bc2, and state that it is unstable until the gate passes.

FENCED: DCR-RECOVERY-ARM (open r15 mandate — do not draft against) — when r15 supplies a fireable recovery continuation, any membership/fate interaction will be appended to the keyed socket register; no current requirement depends on it.
SHALL NOT choose a beamr API, backend replacement, buffering limit, duplicate policy, or resubscription policy in this skeleton.
SHALL NOT replace or take ownership of beamr's scheduler-owned synchronous node-down purge hook.
SHALL NOT merge this backend-ordering retirement into listener readiness, scheduler reply waits, or SDK reader cancellation.
SHALL NOT absorb unrelated growth-section families or claim that the repository sweep certifies dependency-internal wake sources.
verification: MEMBERSHIP QUIESCENCE — implement `stable_membership_source_has_zero_consumer_wakes` with a source arm barrier and flat snapshot/wake counters, using `crates/liminal-server/src/server/connection/process_wake_tests.rs:43-65` as the inversion precedent; paper section `2.7 Requirement — idle-cost lens`, source facts reverified by the banked scout pack at fe3f7ec.
MEMBERSHIP TOMBSTONE — delete the direct `poll_once` test at `crates/liminal-server/src/cluster/membership.rs:391-403`; preserve empty-initial-view coverage through the new atomic source contract.

ORDERING MATRIX — race join/leave across initial handoff, rapid join-leave-rejoin, duplicates/coalescing, source close/error/overflow, and shutdown before/with/after a delta using deterministic barriers.

REAL-CLUSTER INVERSION — rewrite `crates/liminal-server/tests/cluster_two_node.rs:26-39,128-146,228-291` so event barriers replace eventual membership polling while late-join backfill and synchronous node-drop purge assertions remain.

DELETION CHECK — production membership code has no `PollLoop`, `POLL_INTERVAL`, `poll_once`, `run_poll_loop`, stop-flag sampling, snapshot diff cadence, or sleep family identified at `crates/liminal-server/src/cluster/membership.rs:40-44,112-128,198-230`.
DOCUMENT GATE — before dispatch, audit every contract-derived value for `PENDING-CONTRACT-HASH`, confirm the final r15 hash/two-key evidence, and confirm the DCR fenced hole remains non-dependent.
